textSnippets: Technician: Hey Luis, got you all done. The reason that took so long is I just kind of also built all your equipment options when I was out there too.
Technician: I'd love to wrap up with you here.
stageHighlights.analysis: The technician did not properly greet the customer and introduce himself and the company. The technician only greeted the customer at the end of the service call, after the repair had taken place. The greeting of the call when the technician first arrived was not present in the conversation. The technician also does not introduce himself or the company in any detail.

textSnippets: Technician: Yeah, AI's a big thing, man. So let's go right to just the differences here. I just wanted to show you. So remember before, how we had that before, that below freezing temperature?
Technician: At 41. It's good to see.
Technician: And now we have that 20-degree difference as well.
stageHighlights.analysis: The technician observed and reported that the 'below freezing' temperature on the refrigerator was 41 degrees. This implied that the refrigerator was not working and needed to be fixed. Either the technician and customer had discussed this problem beforehand, or the customer had presumably reported that there was some problem with the refrigerator, and the technician then investigated the refrigerator and identified this problem. The technician also mentioned that 'it's an old unit,' i.e. that the refrigerator is old. Therefore the technician implied that a more comprehensive set of replacements/upgrades would be necessary. Overall this is how the technician identified the customer's interview. It is unclear if they discussed this issue before.
